meta.description: Compressed hunt cards for solana — one-line detection rules for bulk scanning
meta.usage: Load cards into context. For each card, grep target code for card.grep. On hit: execute card.check steps directly, use validWhen/invalidWhen/impact for rapid triage, use optional preconditions/proofShape/falsePositiveSignals/reportEvidence when present, and only read full DB entry (card.ref + card.lines) for confirmed true/likely positives.

cards.title: 1. Account Validation Vulnerabilities
cards.detect:
Programs fail to verify that:

1. **Signer**: The account actually signed the transaction

2. **Owner**: The account is...
check: VERIFY: Signal 1: State-changing function lacks onlyOwner/onlyRole modifier; Signal 2: External function accepts arbitrary address and calls interface...
PROVE: Attacker crafts a transaction with malicious accounts; Program accepts accounts without proper validation
FALSIFY: Not this bug when: Function is internal/private and only called from access-controlled paths; Safe if: Function is restricted via onlyOwner/onlyRole/require(msg.sender...
IMPACT: Unauthorized state modifications
All authority accounts verify is_signer
All program-owned accounts verify owner
cards.antipattern: No signer validation - anyone can call as any account
cards.securePattern: Anchor Account type validates owner automatically
cards.validWhen: Signal 1: State-changing function lacks onlyOwner/onlyRole modifier; Signal 2: External function accepts arbitrary address and calls interface methods without registry...
cards.invalidWhen: Not this bug when: Function is internal/private and only called from access-controlled paths; Safe if: Function is restricted via onlyOwner/onlyRole/require(msg.sender...
preconditions: attacker control of: specific conditions per pattern
Attacker crafts a transaction with malicious accounts
Attacker gains unauthorized access to restricted functions
cards.proofShape: Attacker crafts a transaction with malicious accounts -> Program accepts accounts without proper validation -> Attacker gains unauthorized access to restricted functions
falsePositiveSignals: Not this bug when: Function is internal/private and only called from access-controlled paths
Safe if: Function is restricted via onlyOwner/onlyRole/require(msg.sender == ...)
Requires attacker control of: specific conditions per pattern

cards.title: 2. Account Data Reallocation Vulnerabilities
cards.detect:
Programs fail to:

1. Zero-initialize newly allocated memory regions

2. Handle allocation failures gracefully

3....
check: PROVE: Account is reallocated to a larger size; New memory region contains garbage data or sensitive information
FALSIFY: Zero-initialize new memory regions
IMPACT: Uninitialized memory access
All realloc calls use zero_init=true OR manually zero-initialize
All realloc calls handle errors properly
LOOK FOR: realloc(..., false) without manual zero-initialization
cards.antipattern: No error handling for reallocation failure
cards.validWhen: Programs fail to: 1.
cards.proofShape: Account is reallocated to a larger size -> New memory region contains garbage data or sensitive information -> Program reads uninitialized memory as valid data

cards.title: 3. Lamports Transfer from PDA Vulnerabilities
cards.detect: Transferring lamports without checking rent-exempt threshold
check: VERIFY: Transferring lamports without checking rent-exempt threshold
PROVE: Protocol transfers lamports out of PDA without rent check; PDA balance falls below rent-exempt threshold
FALSIFY: Direct lamport manipulation for program-owned accounts
IMPACT: PDA account garbage collection
All lamport deductions from PDAs check rent-exempt balance
Direct lamport manipulation used (not system_program::transfer) for PDAs
cards.antipattern: System program cannot deduct from non-system-owned accounts
cards.securePattern: Direct lamport manipulation for program-owned accounts
cards.validWhen: Transferring lamports without checking rent-exempt threshold
cards.invalidWhen: Direct lamport manipulation for program-owned accounts
preconditions: transferring SOL (lamports) out of a PDA, programs must ensure the PDA maintains rent-exempt status and use the correct transfer...
cards.proofShape: Protocol transfers lamports out of PDA without rent check -> PDA balance falls below rent-exempt threshold -> Account is garbage collected by Solana runtime

cards.title: 4. CPI (Cross-Program Invocation) Vulnerabilities
cards.detect: Arbitrary program invocation without validation
check: VERIFY: Arbitrary program invocation without validation
PROVE: Attacker provides malicious program address; Program performs CPI to untrusted program
FALSIFY: Anchor CPI types ensure correct ordering
IMPACT: Arbitrary code execution via malicious programs
All CPI targets validated against known program IDs
All signer seeds include correct bump value
cards.antipattern: Missing bump value causes signature verification failure
cards.securePattern: Anchor CPI types ensure correct ordering
cards.validWhen: Arbitrary program invocation without validation
cards.invalidWhen: Anchor CPI types ensure correct ordering
cards.impact: Arbitrary code execution via malicious programs
cards.proofShape: Attacker provides malicious program address -> Program performs CPI to untrusted program -> Malicious program executes arbitrary operations

cards.title: 5. Unvalidated Account Vulnerabilities
cards.detect: Token program not validated against expected ID
check: VERIFY: Token program not validated against expected ID
PROVE: Lamports can be donated to uninitialized accounts
FALSIFY: Anchor validates token program automatically
IMPACT: Malicious token program execution
Token program validated against SPL Token/Token-2022 ID
All sysvars use Sysvar<'info, T> type
cards.antipattern: Lamports can be donated to uninitialized accounts
cards.validWhen: Token program not validated against expected ID
cards.proofShape: Lamports can be donated to uninitialized accounts

cards.title: 6. Account Reloading Vulnerabilities
cards.detect: After a CPI that modifies an account, the program continues using the stale local copy instead of refreshing the...
check: VERIFY: After a CPI that modifies an account, the program continues using the stale local copy instead of refreshing the account data.
PROVE: Program loads account state at transaction start; CPI call modifies the account
FALSIFY: Reload account after CPI
IMPACT: Incorrect calculations based on stale data
All accounts modified by CPI are reloaded before subsequent use
State-dependent logic follows reload() calls
cards.antipattern: Using stale balance after transfer
cards.validWhen: After a CPI that modifies an account, the program continues using the stale local copy instead of refreshing the account data.
cards.impact: Incorrect calculations based on stale data
cards.proofShape: Program loads account state at transaction start -> CPI call modifies the account -> Program uses stale state for subsequent logic

cards.title: 7. Account Closure Vulnerabilities
cards.detect: Missing authorization for account closure
check: VERIFY: Missing authorization for account closure
PROVE: No authorization check for closure
FALSIFY: Validate destination matches authority
IMPACT: Unauthorized account closures
All closures verify authority/owner
Destination address validated (usually should be authority)
cards.antipattern: No authorization check for closure
cards.validWhen: Missing authorization for account closure
cards.proofShape: No authorization check for closure

cards.title: 8. DOS Attack Vectors
cards.detect: Using `init` instead of `init_if_needed` for ATA creation
check: VERIFY: Using init instead of init_if_needed for ATA creation
PROVE: Predictable seeds allow front-running
FALSIFY: Handle pre-created accounts with validation
IMPACT: Transaction failures for legitimate users
ATAs use init_if_needed
Predictable PDAs use init_if_needed with validation
cards.antipattern: Predictable seeds allow front-running
cards.validWhen: Using init instead of init_if_needed for ATA creation
cards.proofShape: Predictable seeds allow front-running

cards.title: 9. Mint & Token Extension Vulnerabilities
cards.detect: Not checking for close authority extension
check: VERIFY: Not checking for close authority extension
PROVE: Fee-on-transfer tokens cause accounting errors
FALSIFY: Use transfer_checked for fee-enabled tokens
IMPACT: Tokens become worthless if mint closed
Close authority validated on mints
Freeze authority checked when relevant
cards.antipattern: Fee-on-transfer tokens cause accounting errors
cards.validWhen: Not checking for close authority extension
cards.impact: Tokens become worthless if mint closed
cards.proofShape: Fee-on-transfer tokens cause accounting errors

cards.title: 10. Event Emission Vulnerabilities
cards.detect: Emitting incorrect data in events
check: VERIFY: Emitting incorrect data in events
PROVE: Event reports wrong amount
FALSIFY: Event for critical state change
cards.antipattern: Event reports wrong amount
cards.validWhen: Emitting incorrect data in events
cards.proofShape: Event reports wrong amount

cards.title: 11. Arithmetic and Data Handling Vulnerabilities
cards.detect: Unchecked arithmetic operations
check: VERIFY: Unchecked arithmetic operations
PROVE: Can overflow in release builds
FALSIFY: Check for zero before division
IMPACT: Integer overflow/underflow
cards.antipattern: Can overflow in release builds
cards.validWhen: Unchecked arithmetic operations
cards.proofShape: Can overflow in release builds

cards.title: 12. Seed Collision Vulnerabilities
cards.detect: Simple/short seed prefixes
check: VERIFY: Simple/short seed prefixes
PROVE: Vote sessions and user votes could collide
FALSIFY: Distinct prefixes and full context
IMPACT: Wrong account accessed
Each account type has unique seed prefix
User-specific accounts include user pubkey in seeds
cards.antipattern: Vote sessions and user votes could collide
cards.validWhen: Simple/short seed prefixes
cards.proofShape: Vote sessions and user votes could collide

cards.title: 13. Account Type Confusion Vulnerabilities
cards.detect: Using raw Borsh deserialization instead of Anchor's `try_deserialize`
check: VERIFY: Using raw Borsh deserialization instead of Anchor's try_deserialize
PROVE: Uses Borsh deserialize without discriminator check
FALSIFY: Use Anchor's try_deserialize with discriminator check
cards.antipattern: Uses Borsh deserialize without discriminator check
cards.securePattern: Use Anchor's try_deserialize with discriminator check
cards.validWhen: Using raw Borsh deserialization instead of Anchor's try_deserialize
cards.invalidWhen: Use Anchor's try_deserialize with discriminator check
cards.proofShape: Uses Borsh deserialize without discriminator check

cards.title: 14. Ed25519 Instruction Introspection Vulnerabilities
cards.detect: The Ed25519 program on Solana is flexible - it can read signature, public key, and message from different instruction...
check: VERIFY: Ed25519 program on Solana is flexible - it can read signature, public key, and message from different instruction indexes and offsets.
PROVE: Doesn't validate Ed25519 instruction structure
FALSIFY: Comprehensive Ed25519 verification
cards.antipattern: Doesn't validate Ed25519 instruction structure
cards.validWhen: The Ed25519 program on Solana is flexible - it can read signature, public key, and message from different instruction indexes and offsets.
cards.proofShape: Doesn't validate Ed25519 instruction structure

cards.title: 15. Signature Replay Attack Vulnerabilities
cards.detect: No nonce tracking for signature-based operations
check: VERIFY: No nonce tracking for signature-based operations
PROVE: Same proof can be submitted multiple times
FALSIFY: Reset nonce when updating signing key
cards.antipattern: Same proof can be submitted multiple times
cards.securePattern: Reset nonce when updating signing key
cards.validWhen: No nonce tracking for signature-based operations
cards.invalidWhen: Reset nonce when updating signing key
cards.proofShape: Same proof can be submitted multiple times

cards.title: 16. Unrestricted CPI Vulnerabilities
cards.detect: Accepting arbitrary program IDs for CPI
check: VERIFY: Accepting arbitrary program IDs for CPI
PROVE: Any swap program accepted
FALSIFY: Restrict to allowlisted programs
cards.antipattern: Any swap program accepted
cards.validWhen: Accepting arbitrary program IDs for CPI
preconditions: a program passes signing authority to another program via CPI, it must carefully validate which programs can be called.
cards.proofShape: Any swap program accepted

cards.title: 17. Initialization Front-Running Vulnerabilities
cards.detect: PDA seeds derived from unauthenticated accounts
check: VERIFY: PDA seeds derived from unauthenticated accounts
PROVE: Anyone can become super_admin for any mint
FALSIFY: create_key must sign the transaction
cards.antipattern: Anyone can become super_admin for any mint
cards.validWhen: PDA seeds derived from unauthenticated accounts
cards.proofShape: Anyone can become super_admin for any mint

cards.title: 18. Create Account Pre-Funding DOS
cards.detect: Using `create_account` instead of the more resilient pattern of separate fund, allocate, and assign operations.
check: VERIFY: Using create_account instead of the more resilient pattern of separate fund, allocate, and assign operations.
PROVE: Fails if account already has lamports
FALSIFY: Handles pre-existing lamports
cards.antipattern: Fails if account already has lamports
cards.validWhen: Using create_account instead of the more resilient pattern of separate fund, allocate, and assign operations.
cards.proofShape: Fails if account already has lamports

cards.title: 19. Rent Exemption Validation Errors
cards.detect: Using `account.lamports()` (which includes rent) in comparisons with values that don't include rent.
check: VERIFY: Using account.lamports() (which includes rent) in comparisons with values that don't include rent.
PROVE: Compares lamports (with rent) to reserves (without rent)
FALSIFY: Subtract rent before comparison
cards.antipattern: Compares lamports (with rent) to reserves (without rent)
cards.validWhen: Using account.lamports() (which includes rent) in comparisons with values that don't include rent.
cards.proofShape: Compares lamports (with rent) to reserves (without rent)

cards.title: 20. Liquidation and State Invariant Vulnerabilities
cards.detect: Sentinel encoding mismatch**: treating `0` as “no tick exists” even when tick `0` is valid.
check: VERIFY: Sentinel encoding mismatch: treating 0 as “no tick exists” even when tick 0 is valid.
PROVE: saturation hides overflow and produces underpriced assets
FALSIFY: enforce reserve/token-vault consistency before any early return
Tick sentinel encoding distinguishes "unset" from valid tick 0
All reserve mint and vault_id checks run before path-specific early returns
LOOK FOR: sentinel checks that map 0 -> no value for signed domains where 0 is valid
cards.antipattern: saturation hides overflow and produces underpriced assets
cards.securePattern: enforce reserve/token-vault consistency before any early return
cards.validWhen: Sentinel encoding mismatch: treating 0 as “no tick exists” even when tick 0 is valid.
cards.invalidWhen: enforce reserve/token-vault consistency before any early return
cards.proofShape: saturation hides overflow and produces underpriced assets

cards.title: 21. CPI Policy and Program Upgradeability Bypass
cards.detect: CPI policy stores digest/operator rules for an instruction payload but not upgradeability guarantees for `program_id`.
check: VERIFY: CPI policy stores digest/operator rules for an instruction payload but not upgradeability guarantees for program_id.
PROVE: invoke path relies on stale trust from old digest registration
FALSIFY: reject upgradeable targets unless upgrade authority is None
Every allowlisted CPI target is immutable or has `upgrade_authority == None`
Policy stores `program_id` and rejects digest reuse across different programs
LOOK FOR: CPI digest/allowlist updates that omit checks for bpf_loader_upgradeable ownership
cards.antipattern: invoke path relies on stale trust from old digest registration
cards.securePattern: reject upgradeable targets unless upgrade authority is None
cards.validWhen: CPI policy stores digest/operator rules for an instruction payload but not upgradeability guarantees for program_id.
cards.invalidWhen: reject upgradeable targets unless upgrade authority is None
cards.proofShape: invoke path relies on stale trust from old digest registration

cards.title: 1. MintCloseAuthority Extension Vulnerabilities
cards.detect: When a protocol accepts any Token-2022 mint without checking for the `MintCloseAuthority` extension:
check: VERIFY: Signal 1: Critical input parameter not validated against expected range or format; Signal 2: Oracle data consumed without staleness check or sanity...
PROVE: Create Token-2022 mint with 18 decimals and MintCloseAuthority extension; Initialize protocol configuration using this mint
FALSIFY: Not this bug when: Validation exists but is in an upstream function caller; Safe if: Parameter range is inherently bounded by the type or protocol invariant
IMPACT: Decimal rate calculations become incorrect
Check for MintCloseAuthority extension before accepting mints
Validate that decimals-based calculations cannot be exploited
cards.antipattern: Accepts any mint without checking close authority
cards.validWhen: Signal 1: Critical input parameter not validated against expected range or format; Signal 2: Oracle data consumed without staleness check or sanity bounds
cards.invalidWhen: Not this bug when: Validation exists but is in an upstream function caller; Safe if: Parameter range is inherently bounded by the type or protocol invariant
preconditions: attacker control of: specific conditions per pattern
a protocol accepts any Token-2022 mint without checking for the MintCloseAuthority extension
cards.proofShape: Create Token-2022 mint with 18 decimals and MintCloseAuthority extension -> Initialize protocol configuration using this mint -> Protocol calculates ld2sd_rate = 10^(18-6) = 10^12
falsePositiveSignals: Not this bug when: Validation exists but is in an upstream function caller
Safe if: Parameter range is inherently bounded by the type or protocol invariant
Requires attacker control of: specific conditions per pattern

cards.title: 2. Freeze Authority Vulnerabilities
cards.detect: Protocols accept mints with freeze authority set, allowing the mint authority to:
check: VERIFY: Protocols accept mints with freeze authority set, allowing the mint authority to
PROVE: Attacker creates mint with freeze authority; Attacker deposits tokens into protocol vault
FALSIFY: Check freeze authority before accepting mint
IMPACT: Protocol token accounts become frozen
All accepted mints checked for freeze_authority == None
Consider allowlisting specific mints for high-value protocols
cards.antipattern: Escrow can be frozen, blocking all bridge operations
cards.securePattern: Check freeze authority before accepting mint
cards.validWhen: Protocols accept mints with freeze authority set, allowing the mint authority to
cards.invalidWhen: Check freeze authority before accepting mint
preconditions: Attacker creates mint with freeze authority
Attacker deposits tokens into protocol vault
Attacker freezes the protocol's token vault
All users' funds in the vault become permanently locked
cards.proofShape: Attacker creates mint with freeze authority -> Attacker deposits tokens into protocol vault -> Attacker freezes the protocol's token vault

cards.title: 3. Transfer Fee Extension Vulnerabilities
cards.detect: Protocols assume `transfer(amount)` results in recipient receiving exactly `amount`.
check: VERIFY: Protocols assume transfer(amount) results in recipient receiving exactly amount.
PROVE: Doesn't account for transfer fees
FALSIFY: Explicitly reject transfer fee tokens
IMPACT: Protocol under-records actual token balances
Check for TransferFeeConfig extension on accepted mints
Use balance-before/after pattern for fee tokens
cards.antipattern: Doesn't account for transfer fees
cards.validWhen: Protocols assume transfer(amount) results in recipient receiving exactly amount.
cards.proofShape: Doesn't account for transfer fees

cards.title: 4. Permanent Delegate Extension Vulnerabilities
cards.detect:
The permanent delegate can:

1. Transfer tokens from any token account holding that mint

2. Burn tokens from any...
check: VERIFY: permanent delegate can
PROVE: Permanent delegate can drain vault
FALSIFY: Reject mints with permanent delegate
IMPACT: Permanent delegate can drain all protocol vaults
cards.antipattern: Permanent delegate can drain vault
cards.validWhen: The permanent delegate can
cards.impact: Permanent delegate can drain all protocol vaults
preconditions: wait for large deposits then drain
cards.proofShape: Permanent delegate can drain vault

cards.title: 5. Token Account Size Vulnerabilities
cards.detect: Hardcoding `space = 165` for token accounts fails for Token-2022 because:
check: VERIFY: Hardcoded size fails for Token-2022
FALSIFY: Let Anchor/Token program handle sizing
cards.antipattern: Hardcoded size fails for Token-2022
cards.validWhen: Hardcoded size fails for Token-2022

cards.title: 6. Default Account State Extension Vulnerabilities
cards.detect: Doesn't handle frozen default state
check: VERIFY: Doesn't handle frozen default state
FALSIFY: Validate default state is not frozen
cards.antipattern: Doesn't handle frozen default state
cards.securePattern: Validate default state is not frozen
cards.validWhen: Doesn't handle frozen default state
cards.invalidWhen: Validate default state is not frozen

cards.title: 7. Transfer Hook Extension Vulnerabilities
cards.detect:
Transfer hooks can:

1. Reject transfers arbitrarily

2. Cause DOS by reverting

3. Introduce reentrancy through...
check: VERIFY: Transfer hooks can: 1.
PROVE: Hook can cause unexpected failures
FALSIFY: Validate transfer hook presence
cards.antipattern: Hook can cause unexpected failures
cards.validWhen: Transfer hooks can: 1.
cards.proofShape: Hook can cause unexpected failures

cards.title: 8. Interest Bearing Extension Vulnerabilities
cards.detect:
With interest-bearing tokens:

1. `token_account.amount` returns the base amount, not the actual balance with...
check: VERIFY: With interest-bearing tokens
PROVE: Doesn't account for accrued interest
FALSIFY: Reject if protocol can't handle interest calculations
LOOK FOR: Direct reads of token_account.amount without interest adjustment
cards.antipattern: Doesn't account for accrued interest
cards.securePattern: Reject if protocol can't handle interest calculations
cards.validWhen: With interest-bearing tokens
cards.invalidWhen: Reject if protocol can't handle interest calculations
cards.proofShape: Doesn't account for accrued interest

With confidential transfers:

2. Traditional balance...

cards.detect:

2. Fail to follow the metadata pointer to...

cards.detect:

2. Attempt restricted CPI operations...

FALSIFY: Check for CPI Guard and handle appropriately
